title: leichhardtia flavida
intro: Leichhardtia flavida, also known as the Yellow Milk Tree, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow latex that flows from its leaves when injured. The medicinal properties of Leichhardtia flavida are attributed to its latex, which contains flavinoids, glycosides, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Leichhardtia flavida are often made by drying and crushing the leaves, then infusing them in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
